content: Hi, I'm Logan. I'm a chef and restaurant owner, and I'm really passionate about cooking and trying new recipes. I recently went through a career change from journalism, which was a big adjustment but I'm loving my new career.
Nice to meet you, Logan! It's great that you're pursuing your passion for cooking. What inspired you to make the career change from journalism?

I think I just got burned out from the journalism world and I wanted to try something new. I've always loved cooking and I figured, why not give it a shot? I'm also really into photography and I love taking pictures of the dishes I create.
That's a great attitude to have! Photography is a wonderful hobby. Do you have any favorite dishes that you like to cook or photograph?

Yeah, I love cooking all sorts of things, but I think my favorite dish to cook is probably a good old-fashioned burger. I'm also really into beekeeping and I've been keeping a few hives in my backyard for a few years now.
Beekeeping is such a unique hobby! What got you into it?

I've always been interested in sustainability and the environment, and I think beekeeping is a great way to contribute to that. Plus, it's just so fascinating to learn about the bees and how they work together. I'm also really into salsa dancing and I've been taking classes at a local studio for about 3 years now.
Salsa dancing is a great way to stay active and have fun! Do you have a favorite type of salsa music or dance style?

Yeah, I love all types of salsa music, but I think my favorite is probably Cuban salsa. I've been listening to a lot of Celia Cruz and Tito Puente lately. I'm also really into mushroom foraging and I've been going on foraging trips with my friend, Rachel, who's an expert in the field.
Celia Cruz and Tito Puente are legends! Mushroom foraging is also really interesting. What kind of mushrooms do you like to forage for?

I love foraging for all sorts of mushrooms, but I think my favorite is probably the chanterelle. They're just so delicious and versatile. I'm planning to go on a foraging trip in the Redwood Forest next month, which I'm really looking forward to.

Chanterelles are amazing! The Redwood Forest is such a beautiful place to forage for mushrooms. I'm sure you'll have a great time. Do you have any plans for your restaurant or cooking career in the near future?

Yeah, I'm thinking of starting a cooking class or workshop series, maybe focusing on sustainable cooking or foraging. I've been talking to my business partner, Michael, about the idea and he's really on board.
